title: How to Reset Your CloudDash API Key
content:
CloudDash API keys provide programmatic access to your monitoring data, alert configurations, and dashboard settings. If your key has been exposed, compromised, or you need to rotate credentials as part of your security policy, you can reset it from the CloudDash console without downtime to existing integrations.

To reset your API key, log in to CloudDash and navigate to Settings > API & Integrations > API Keys. Locate the key you wish to reset and click the three-dot menu, then select 'Rotate Key'. CloudDash generates a new key immediately while keeping the old key valid for a 24-hour grace period. This grace period allows you to update your CI/CD pipelines, Terraform configurations, and monitoring scripts without service interruption.

After rotation, update the CLOUDDASH_API_KEY environment variable in all systems that call the CloudDash REST API. Our official SDKs for Python, Node.js, and Go read this variable automatically when initialized with default settings. Verify the new key by running a test request: GET https://api.clouddash.io/v1/health with the Authorization header set to Bearer YOUR_NEW_KEY.

Organization admins can enforce mandatory key rotation every 90 days under Settings > Security > API Key Policy. When enabled, users receive email reminders 14 days before expiration. Keys that exceed the policy window are automatically revoked. For Enterprise customers, audit log entries (KB-019) record every key rotation event with the acting user's identity and timestamp.

If you lose access to the console, contact your organization admin or submit a support ticket with verified account ownership. Never share API keys in Slack, email, or public repositories. Use CloudDash's scoped keys feature to limit permissions to read-only monitoring data when full write access is not required.
